title: A broad-spectrum lasso peptide antibiotic targeting the bacterial ribosome
objective: Identify and characterize lariocidin, a novel ribosome-targeting lasso peptide antibiotic, and elucidate its mechanism of action and antimicrobial properties against Gram-negative and Gram-positive bacterial pathogens
problem_statement: Antibiotic resistance is a global crisis responsible for millions of deaths, particularly against Gram-negative bacteria. New antibiotics with novel mechanisms of action targeting previously unexploited ribosomal sites are urgently needed. Lasso peptides, despite having reported antibacterial properties, have never been shown to inhibit the ribosome, one of the main antibiotic targets

procedure: Grew bacteria on soil-agar plates at room temperature for approximately one year
Extended incubation period to reveal slow-growing, frequently overlooked bacteria
Regrew individual colonies in rich medium
Prepared methanolic extracts and partially fractionated by reversed-phase chromatography
Tested against clinical multi-drug resistant (MDR) Gram-negative pathogen Acinetobacter baumannii C0286

lariocidin_discovery.type: Lasso peptide (RiPP with structurally constrained knotted fold)

target_pathogens: Other clinically relevant Gram-negative and Gram-positive pathogens

mechanism_of_action.binding_site: Unique site in small ribosomal subunit
mechanism_of_action.mechanism_novelty: First lasso peptide shown to inhibit ribosomal function

ribosomal_binding_characteristics.binding_site_novelty: New ribosomal site distinct from well-characterized functional sites
ribosomal_binding_characteristics.comparison_to_known_antibiotics: Different from decoding centre, peptidyl transferase centre, and nascent peptide exit tunnel
ribosomal_binding_characteristics.resistance_development: Novel site and mechanism result in low propensity for spontaneous resistance

spontaneous_resistant_mutants.identification_method: Plating on solid medium supplemented with LAR

initial_mutations.genes_affected: Genes related to electron transport chain and respiration
initial_mutations.findings: Whole-genome sequencing identified mutations in aerobic respiration genes

mechanism_clarification.findings: Deletion of individual identified genes showed no significant MIC changes
mechanism_clarification.interpretation: Aerobic respiration/electron transport chain alterations influence LAR uptake but are not direct targets
mechanism_clarification.evidence: LAR exhibits 4-8 fold increase in MIC under anaerobic conditions

ribosomal_mutations.challenge: Redundancy of rRNA genes in bacterial genomes (10 rrn alleles in B. subtilis, 7 in E. coli) prevents direct isolation of target site mutations
ribosomal_mutations.solution: Used E. coli strain SQ110DTC with single rRNA operon

pharmacological_properties.resistance_propensity: Low propensity for generating spontaneous resistance
pharmacological_properties.in_vivo_efficacy: Potent in vivo activity in mouse model of Acinetobacter baumannii infection

clinical_significance.gap_addressed: Novel mechanism targeting new ribosomal site avoids common resistance mechanisms
clinical_significance.therapeutic_potential: Alternative protein-synthesis inhibitor with novel chemical scaffold
clinical_significance.drug_development: Foundation for development of much-needed antibacterial drugs
clinical_significance.resistance_crisis: Addresses urgent need for new antibiotics in era of antibiotic resistance

lasso_peptide_antibiotic_landscape.lariocidin_novelty: First lasso peptide shown to inhibit ribosome

research_impact.discovery_significance: Demonstrates lasso peptides can target previously unexploited ribosomal sites
research_impact.chemical_scaffold: Novel lasso peptide scaffold for antibiotic development
research_impact.future_applications: Prototype for developing ribosome-targeting lasso peptide antibiotics
research_impact.antimicrobial_development: Opens new routes for discovery of alternative protein-synthesis inhibitors
